atoms.content: Pandas is a data analysis library built on top of NumPy. It provides DataFrames, which are powerful 2D tables with labeled axes for easy data manipulation.
Pandas is like Microsoft Excel for Python, but it can handle millions of rows instantly and is controlled entirely by code instead of a mouse.
A DataFrame represents a tabular, spreadsheet-like data structure containing an ordered collection of columns, each of which can be a different value type.
A Series is a single column extracted from a DataFrame. It is essentially a 1D NumPy array with an index attached to it.

1. DataFrame: 2D table.
2. Series: 1D column.
3. Built for cleaning, grouping, and filtering tabular data.
What is the primary 2D data structure used in Pandas called? (Answer: DataFrame).
pd.read_csv() loads a CSV file into a DataFrame — the first thing you do in any ML project. Use df.to_csv() to save results back to disk.
Use .head() to preview the first 5 rows, .info() to see column types and missing values, .describe() for summary statistics, and .shape for dimensions.
Filter rows with boolean indexing: df[df['age'] > 30]. Use .groupby() to aggregate data by categories — essential for feature engineering.
